Here's a summary of what I found about ScrapFly's Extraction API for Twitter/X.com structured data in 2026:

## ScrapFly API for Twitter/X.com Data Extraction

### Background: Why Scraping is Needed
X.com (formerly Twitter) killed its free API in 2023, and since then the platform has been rolling out defensive changes every 2-4 weeks that break DIY scrapers. The official paid API now starts at $42,000 per year for basic access to 100 tweets.

### ScrapFly's Extraction API
ScrapFly's Extraction API allows extraction of structured data from any text content such as HTML, Text, Markdown using AI, LLM, and custom parsing instructions. Key capabilities include:

- **LLM-Powered Extraction:** Extract clean, structured data like JSON or CSV from any document using the power of LLMs.
- **Three Extraction Methods:** Three extraction methods handle different use cases: precise template-based extraction, natural language LLM prompts, and automatic AI models for common structures.
- **Custom Templates:** You can define an extraction template to get structured data, using either an ephemeral template (declared on the fly on the API call) or a stored template (declared in the dashboard).

### Social Media Post Auto-Extraction Model
ScrapFly offers a dedicated **social media post** automatic extraction model that returns structured fields including:
The social media platform where the post was made (e.g., facebook, instagram, twitter, linkedIn, tiktok) in lowercase. The schema includes fields for post ID, author information (ID, name, username/handle, profile URL), text content of the post, engagement metrics including likes, comments, shares/retweets, and views.

### Twitter/X.com Scraping with ScrapFly
Twitter/X data can be extracted using their GraphQL API endpoints. The basic profile scraping extracts structured data from JSON-LD markup.

ScrapFly's maintained scraper handles common Twitter challenges:
- Guest tokens are handled automatically, and doc_ids are tracked and updated within 24 hours of rotation.
- Residential proxies are included, and rate limiting and anti-bot bypass are built in.

### Pricing & Integration
- Most projects start at $20-100/month depending on request volume.
- The Extraction HTTP API can be accessed in any HTTP client like curl, httpie, or any HTTP client library in any programming language, with first-class support via Python and Typescript SDKs.
- Scrapfly integrates AI-powered data extraction directly into the Web Scraping API, combining fetching and extraction in a single API call.

Here's a summary of the **ScrapFly API scrape endpoint response format and result fields** based on the documentation:

## Response Format

By default, the API responds in JSON. A more efficient msgpack format is also available by setting the `accept: application/msgpack` header.

The default response format is JSON, and the scraped content is available in `result.content`. Your scrape configuration is present in `config`, and other activated feature information is available in `context`.

## Top-Level Response Structure

The JSON response contains three main sections:
- **`result`** – The scrape result data
- **`config`** – The scrape configuration used
- **`context`** – Additional feature/context information (including cost info)

## `result` Fields

From the Python SDK source code, the `result` object contains the following fields:

The scrape result includes: `content`, `log_url`, `status`, `success`, `duration`, `format`, `screenshots`, `dns`, `ssl`, `iframes`, `browser_data`, and `error`.

Additionally, from the API response handler code:

The `result` object includes fields such as `request_headers`, `status`, `success`, `response_headers`, `status_code`, `reason`, `format`, and `content`.

### Key `result` Fields Explained:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| **`content`** | The scraped page content (HTML, text, markdown, etc.) |
| **`status`** | Scrape status (e.g., `"DONE"`) |
| **`success`** | Boolean indicating if the scrape was successful |
| **`status_code`** | HTTP status code from the upstream website |
| **`reason`** | HTTP reason phrase |
| **`format`** | Response content type — indicated in `result.format` field, it can be `TEXT` or `BINARY` (also `blob` or `clob` for large objects) |
| **`duration`** | Scrape request duration |
| **`request_headers`** | Headers sent in the scrape request |
| **`response_headers`** | Headers received from the upstream website |
| **`log_url`** | URL to view the scrape log in the dashboard |
| **`screenshots`** | Screenshot data (if enabled) |
| **`browser_data`** | Browser data including XHR calls (if `render_js` enabled) |
| **`dns`** | DNS resolution info (optional) |
| **`ssl`** | SSL/TLS certificate info (optional) |
| **`iframes`** | Iframe content (if captured) |
| **`error`** | Error details if the scrape failed |
| **`extracted_data`** | When using data extraction, extracted data is available in `result.extracted_data`. |

## Large Objects (CLOB/BLOB)

Large text responses greater than 5MB are called "CLOB" and binary are called "BLOB" — these are offloaded from the API response. Instead of the actual content in `response.result.content`, you get a URL to download the large object.

`response.result.format` indicates whether it's a large object by checking if it's `blob` or `clob`, and `response.result.content` contains the URL to download the content.

## Content Encoding

Text content is returned as UTF-8 while binary is encoded in base64, so you can scrape any kind of data (PDF, ZIP, etc.). Gzip compression is available through the `content-encoding: gzip` header.

## Error Structure

If there's an error, the `result.error` field contains a structure with `code`, `message`, `retryable`, `http_code`, and `links`.

## Useful Response Headers

The cost results are available in the `X-Scrapfly-Api-Cost` API response header and the `context.cost` data field.

For full details, visit the official documentation at [scrapfly.io/docs/scrape-api/getting-started](https://scrapfly.io/docs/scrape-api/getting-started).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

async def _scrapfly_fetch(url: str, api_key: str) -> str:
    """Call ScrapFly API and return rendered HTML."""
    params = {
        "url": url,
        "key": api_key,
        "asp": "true",
        "render_js": "true",
        "country": "us",
    }

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""X.com content fetcher using ScrapFly API. Handles tweets and X Articles."""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

import httpx
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from loguru import logger

_SCRAPFLY_API = "https://api.scrapfly.io/scrape"
_SCRAPFLY_TIMEOUT = 160.0  # ScrapFly default read timeout is 155s


class ScrapFlyError(Exception):
    """Raised when ScrapFly API call fails."""


@dataclass
class TweetContent:
    tweet_id: str
    author_name: str
    author_username: str
    text: str
    follower_count: int | None = None  # None = not available from HTML
    is_verified: bool | None = None  # None = not available from HTML
    embedded_urls: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class ArticleContent:
    url: str
    title: str | None
    author_name: str | None
    body: str


_TWEET_URL_RE = re.compile(r"x\.com/(\w+)/status/(\d+)")
_ARTICLE_URL_RE = re.compile(r"x\.com/i/article/")
_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")


def detect_content_type(url: str) -> str:
    """Return 'tweet', 'article', or 'unknown'."""
    if _TWEET_URL_RE.search(url):
        return "tweet"
    if _ARTICLE_URL_RE.search(url):
        return "article"
    return "unknown"


def extract_tweet_id(url: str) -> str | None:
    match = _TWEET_URL_RE.search(url)
    return match.group(2) if match else None


def _extract_author_username(url: str) -> str:
    """Extract author username from tweet URL."""
    match = _TWEET_URL_RE.search(url)
    return match.group(1) if match else "unknown"


async def _scrapfly_fetch(url: str, api_key: str) -> str:
    """Call ScrapFly API and return rendered HTML."""
    params = {
        "url": url,
        "key": api_key,
        "asp": "true",
        "render_js": "true",
        "country": "us",
    }
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=_SCRAPFLY_TIMEOUT) as client:
        response = await client.get(_SCRAPFLY_API, params=params)

    if response.status_code != 200:
        raise ScrapFlyError(f"ScrapFly returned {response.status_code}: {response.text[:200]}")

    data = response.json()
    result = data.get("result", {})
    html: str = result.get("content", "")
    if not html:
        raise ScrapFlyError("ScrapFly returned empty content")

    return html


def _parse_tweet_html(html: str, tweet_id: str, url: str) -> TweetContent:
    """Parse rendered tweet page HTML into TweetContent."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "lxml")
    author_username = _extract_author_username(url)

    # Primary: data-testid selectors
    tweet_text_el = soup.select_one('[data-testid="tweetText"]')
    text = tweet_text_el.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True) if tweet_text_el else ""

    # Author display name from User-Name testid
    user_name_el = soup.select_one('[data-testid="User-Name"]')
    author_name = user_name_el.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True) if user_name_el else ""
    # Clean up: User-Name often contains both display name and @handle
    if author_name and f"@{author_username}" in author_name:
        author_name = author_name.split(f"@{author_username}")[0].strip()

    # Fallback: extract from <article> elements
    if not text:
        logger.warning("Primary tweet selectors failed, falling back to <article>")
        articles = soup.find_all("article")
        texts = [a.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True) for a in articles]
        text = " ".join(texts)

    if not author_name:
        author_name = author_username

    embedded_urls = _URL_RE.findall(text)

    return TweetContent(
        tweet_id=tweet_id,
        author_name=author_name,
        author_username=author_username,
        text=text,
        embedded_urls=embedded_urls,
    )


async def fetch_tweet(tweet_id: str, api_key: str) -> TweetContent:
    """Fetch tweet content via ScrapFly API."""
    url = f"https://x.com/i/status/{tweet_id}"
    logger.info(f"Fetching tweet {tweet_id} via ScrapFly")

    html = await _scrapfly_fetch(url, api_key)
    return _parse_tweet_html(html, tweet_id, url)


async def fetch_article(url: str, api_key: str) -> ArticleContent:
    """Fetch X Article via ScrapFly API."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching X article via ScrapFly: {url}")

    html = await _scrapfly_fetch(url, api_key)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "lxml")

    # Title from og:title or <title>
    og_title = soup.find("meta", property="og:title")
    title: str | None = None
    if og_title and hasattr(og_title, "get") and og_title.get("content"):  # type: ignore[union-attr]
        title = str(og_title["content"])  # type: ignore[index]
    if not title:
        title_el = soup.find("title")
        title = title_el.get_text(strip=True) if title_el else None

    # Body from article or main content
    article_el = soup.find("article")
    if article_el:
        body = article_el.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True)
    else:
        main_el = soup.find("main")
        body = main_el.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True) if main_el else soup.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True)

    return ArticleContent(
        url=url,
        title=title,
        author_name=None,
        body=body,
    )

"""X.com content fetcher using ScrapFly API. Handles tweets and X Articles."""

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

import httpx
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from loguru import logger

_SCRAPFLY_API = "https://api.scrapfly.io/scrape"
_SCRAPFLY_TIMEOUT = 160.0  # ScrapFly default read timeout is 155s


class ScrapFlyError(Exception):
    """Raised when ScrapFly API call fails."""


@dataclass
class TweetContent:
    tweet_id: str
    author_name: str
    author_username: str
    text: str
    follower_count: int | None = None  # None = not available from HTML
    is_verified: bool | None = None  # None = not available from HTML
    embedded_urls: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class ArticleContent:
    url: str
    title: str | None
    author_name: str | None
    body: str


_TWEET_URL_RE = re.compile(r"x\.com/(\w+)/status/(\d+)")
_ARTICLE_URL_RE = re.compile(r"x\.com/i/article/")
_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")


def detect_content_type(url: str) -> str:
    """Return 'tweet', 'article', or 'unknown'."""
    if _TWEET_URL_RE.search(url):
        return "tweet"
    if _ARTICLE_URL_RE.search(url):
        return "article"
    return "unknown"


def extract_tweet_id(url: str) -> str | None:
    match = _TWEET_URL_RE.search(url)
    return match.group(2) if match else None


def _extract_author_username(url: str) -> str:
    """Extract author username from tweet URL."""
    match = _TWEET_URL_RE.search(url)
    return match.group(1) if match else "unknown"


async def _scrapfly_fetch(url: str, api_key: str) -> str:
    """Call ScrapFly API and return rendered HTML."""
    params = {
        "url": url,
        "key": api_key,
        "asp": "true",
        "render_js": "true",
        "country": "us",
    }
    logger.info(f"ScrapFly request: url={url}")
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=_SCRAPFLY_TIMEOUT) as client:
        response = await client.get(_SCRAPFLY_API, params=params)

    logger.info(
        f"ScrapFly response: status={response.status_code} "
        f"content_length={len(response.content)}"
    )

    if response.status_code != 200:
        logger.error(f"ScrapFly error body: {response.text[:500]}")
        raise ScrapFlyError(f"ScrapFly returned {response.status_code}: {response.text[:200]}")

    data = response.json()
    result = data.get("result", {})

    # Log ScrapFly result metadata (everything except the HTML content itself)
    log_url = result.get("log_url")
    status = result.get("status")
    status_code = result.get("status_code")
    success = result.get("success")
    duration = result.get("duration")
    fmt = result.get("format")
    error = result.get("error")
    logger.info(
        f"ScrapFly result: status={status} success={success} "
        f"upstream_status={status_code} duration={duration} format={fmt} "
        f"log_url={log_url}"
    )
    if error:
        logger.warning(f"ScrapFly result.error: {error}")

    html: str = result.get("content", "")
    if not html:
        logger.error(f"ScrapFly returned empty content. Full result keys: {list(result.keys())}")
        raise ScrapFlyError("ScrapFly returned empty content")

    logger.debug(f"ScrapFly HTML preview (first 500 chars): {html[:500]}")
    return html


def _parse_tweet_html(html: str, tweet_id: str, url: str) -> TweetContent:
    """Parse rendered tweet page HTML into TweetContent."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "lxml")
    author_username = _extract_author_username(url)

    # Primary: data-testid selectors
    tweet_text_el = soup.select_one('[data-testid="tweetText"]')
    text = tweet_text_el.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True) if tweet_text_el else ""

    # Author display name from User-Name testid
    user_name_el = soup.select_one('[data-testid="User-Name"]')
    author_name = user_name_el.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True) if user_name_el else ""
    # Clean up: User-Name often contains both display name and @handle
    if author_name and f"@{author_username}" in author_name:
        author_name = author_name.split(f"@{author_username}")[0].strip()

    # Fallback: extract from <article> elements
    if not text:
        logger.warning("Primary tweet selectors failed, falling back to <article>")
        articles = soup.find_all("article")
        texts = [a.get_text(separator=" ", strip=True) for a in articles]
        text = " ".join(texts)

    if not author_name:
        author_name = author_username

    embedded_urls = _URL_RE.findall(text)

    return TweetContent(
        tweet_id=tweet_id,
        author_name=author_name,
        author_username=author_username,
        text=text,
        embedded_urls=embedded_urls,
    )


async def fetch_tweet(tweet_id: str, api_key: str) -> TweetContent:
    """Fetch tweet content via ScrapFly API."""
    url = f"https://x.com/i/status/{tweet_id}"
    logger.info(f"Fetching tweet {tweet_id} via ScrapFly")

    html = await _scrapfly_fetch(url, api_key)
    return _parse_tweet_html(html, tweet_id, url)


async def fetch_article(url: str, api_key: str) -> ArticleContent:
    """Fetch X Article via ScrapFly API."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching X article via ScrapFly: {url}")

    html = await _scrapfly_fetch(url, api_key)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "lxml")

    # Title from og:title or <title>
    og_title = soup.find("meta", property="og:title")
    title: str | None = None
    if og_title and hasattr(og_title, "get") and og_title.get("content"):  # type: ignore[union-attr]
        title = str(og_title["content"])  # type: ignore[index]
    if not title:
        title_el = soup.find("title")
        title = title_el.get_text(strip=True) if title_el else None

    # Body from article or main content
    article_el = soup.find("article")
    if article_el:
        body = article_el.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True)
    else:
        main_el = soup.find("main")
        body = main_el.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True) if main_el else soup.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True)

    return ArticleContent(
        url=url,
        title=title,
        author_name=None,
        body=body,
    )

content.text:
Hotovo. Přidal jsem do `bot/fetcher/twitter.py` detailní logování ScrapFly komunikace:

1. **Request** — loguje URL, který se posílá do ScrapFly
2. **Response** — HTTP status + velikost odpovědi
3. **Result metadata** — `status`, `success`, `upstream_status`, `duration`, `format`, `log_url` (odkaz na ScrapFly dashboard log)
4. **Error** — pokud ScrapFly vrátí chybu v `result.error`, loguje se jako warning
5. **Prázdný content** — loguje klíče v `result` pro debug
6. **HTML preview** — prvních 500 znaků na úrovni DEBUG
7. **Parsed tweet** — shrnutí co se vyextrahovalo: autor, délka textu, embedded URLs, followers, verified

Až teď pošleš tweet, v logu na serveru uvidíš přesně co ScrapFly vrátil a co se z toho naparsovalo. To by mělo ukázat proč ten zdroj dostal "účet bez followerů a neověřený" — pravděpodobně protože z HTML se `follower_count` a `is_verified` vůbec neextrahují (jsou vždy `None`).

file.content:
"""Entry point. Wires config, queue, handlers, and starts the Telegram bot."""

import asyncio
import functools
import sys

from loguru import logger
from telegram.ext import ApplicationBuilder, filters
from telegram.ext import MessageHandler as TGMessageHandler

from bot.analyzer.pipeline import run_pipeline
from bot.config import load_config
from bot.notion.projects import ProjectsCache
from bot.notion.writer import NotionWriter
from bot.telegram.formatter import format_result
from bot.telegram.handler import MessageHandler, process_queue

# --- Logging setup ---
logger.remove()
logger.add(sys.stderr, level="INFO", format="{time:YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss} | {level} | {message}")
logger.add("logs/bot.log", rotation="10 MB", retention="7 days", level="DEBUG")
logger.add("logs/errors.log", rotation="10 MB", retention="7 days", level="ERROR")


async def main() -> None:
    config = load_config()
    logger.info("Configuration loaded.")

    writer = NotionWriter(
        notion_api_key=config.notion_api_key,
        rnd_page_id=config.notion_rnd_page_id,
    )
    projects = ProjectsCache(
        notion_api_key=config.notion_api_key,
        projects_page_id=config.notion_projects_page_id,
    )

    pipeline_fn = functools.partial(run_pipeline, config=config, writer=writer, projects=projects)

    queue: asyncio.Queue = asyncio.Queue()
    handler = MessageHandler(queue)

    app = ApplicationBuilder().token(config.telegram_bot_token).build()

    # Listen to messages in the configured group only
    app.add_handler(
        TGMessageHandler(
            filters.Chat(config.telegram_group_id) & filters.TEXT,
            handler.handle,
        )
    )

    # Start the queue processor as a background task
    async with app:
        await app.start()
        logger.info("Bot started. Listening for messages...")

        queue_task = asyncio.create_task(
            process_queue(
                queue=queue,
                pipeline_fn=pipeline_fn,
                format_fn=format_result,
            )
        )

        await app.updater.start_polling(drop_pending_updates=True)

        try:
            await asyncio.Event().wait()  # run forever
        except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit):
            logger.info("Shutting down...")
        finally:
            queue_task.cancel()
            await app.updater.stop()
            await app.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

Bot loguje na dvě místa:

1. **Soubory** v `~/knowledge-source-triage-bot/logs/`
   - `logs/bot.log` — všechno (DEBUG+), rotace po 10 MB, 7 dní
   - `logs/errors.log` — jen chyby

2. **Systemd journal** (stderr → journal)

Na serveru stačí spustit:

```bash
# Nejjednodušší — přímo log soubor:
cat ~/knowledge-source-triage-bot/logs/bot.log | tail -50

# Nebo přes systemd:
sudo journalctl -u triage-bot.service -n 50 --no-pager
```

Chceš abych přidal do deploy workflow krok, který ti pošle poslední logy do Telegramu nebo někam, kde si je přečteš snáz? Nebo mi sem prostě vlož výstup z `tail -50 logs/bot.log` a podívám se na to.

message.content: Co je tail?

content.text:
`tail` je Linux příkaz, který zobrazí poslední řádky souboru. `tail -50` = posledních 50 řádků.

Ale nemusíš to řešit přes terminál. Připoj se na server a pošli mi obsah logu — stačí:

1. Otevři terminál (na Macu Terminal, na Windows PowerShell)
2. Připoj se na server:
   ```
   ssh ubuntu@<IP adresa serveru>
   ```
3. Zobraz posledních 50 řádků logu:
   ```
   tail -50 ~/knowledge-source-triage-bot/logs/bot.log
   ```
4. Zkopíruj výstup a vlož sem

IP adresu serveru najdeš v GitHub secrets jako `DEPLOY_HOST`. Pokud nevíš heslo/klíč — máš SSH klíč, kterým se na server dostaneš?

message.content: Dostane se k tomu logu na serveru claude z desktopove app, kdyz mu dam cestu?

content.text:
Ano, pokud z desktopové Claude Code app otevřeš stejný projekt (`knowledge-source-triage-bot`), můžeš mu říct:

```
Přečti soubor /tmp/session-log.jsonl
```

nebo přímo originál:

```
Přečti /root/.claude/projects/-home-user-knowledge-source-triage-bot/d404abc1-3ef2-4d94-be24-10df8c5c2bea.jsonl
```

Claude na desktopu má přístup ke stejnému filesystému, takže to přečte bez problémů. Jen pozor — `/tmp` se může promazat po restartu, originál v `~/.claude/projects/` je trvalejší.

content.text:
Přesně tak — Claude Code na tom serveru neběží jako `root`, ale jako uživatel `ubuntu`. Takže logy budou v `/home/ubuntu/.claude/projects/...`.

Řekni mu ať zkusí:

```
ssh ... "ls /home/ubuntu/.claude/projects/"
```

To ukáže, jaké projektové adresáře tam existují, a pak se můžeš podívat dovnitř na konkrétní JSONL soubory.

Ale jinak — k čemu potřebuješ ten log? Pokud chceš vědět, co jsem dělal v téhle session, můžu ti to rovnou říct, protože mám celý kontext tady.

```
/root/.claude/projects/-home-user-knowledge-source-triage-bot/4f0d875b-3ddc-4914-95b2-e7edc72405c0.jsonl
```

Tvůj desktop app se připojuje přes SSH jako `ubuntu`, takže nemá přístup k `/root/`. Řekni mu v desktopové app:

```
ssh ... "sudo cat /root/.claude/projects/-home-user-knowledge-source-triage-bot/4f0d875b-3ddc-4914-95b2-e7edc72405c0.jsonl"
```

Klíčové je to `sudo` — bez něj se k `/root/` nedostane. Předtím ten příkaz `sudo` měl, ale používal špatné UUID (`d404abc1...` — to je předchozí session).

content.text:
Soubor existuje (2MB, 514 řádků). Problém je, že desktop Claude se přes SSH přihlašuje jako `ubuntu`, a i se `sudo cat` to nefunguje — pravděpodobně `sudo` vyžaduje heslo nebo je jinak omezené.

Dvě možnosti jak to dostat na desktop:

1. **Přes git** — zkopíruju log do repo, pushnu, desktop si pullne
2. **Opravit SSH přístup** — zkopíruju soubor tam, kam se ubuntu dostane

Která varianta? Nebo chceš, abych prostě řekl, **co přesně má desktop Claude zadat**, aby to fungovalo? Např.:

```
ssh ... "sudo cp /root/.claude/projects/-home-user-knowledge-source-triage-bot/4f0d875b-3ddc-4914-95b2-e7edc72405c0.jsonl /tmp/session-log.jsonl && sudo chmod 644 /tmp/session-log.jsonl" && scp ... ubuntu@130.61.130.58:/tmp/session-log.jsonl ~/session-log.jsonl
```
